    96→@dataclass(repr=False, eq=False)
    97→class ZWaveJSLock(BaseLock):
    98→    """Class to represent ZWave JS lock."""
    99→
   100→    lock_config_entry: ConfigEntry = field(repr=False)
   101→    _listeners: list[Callable[[], None]] = field(init=False, default_factory=list)
   102→    _value_update_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = field(init=False, default=None)
   103→    _push_retry_cancel: Callable[[], None] | None = field(init=False, default=None)
   104→    _set_in_progress_code_slot: int | None = field(init=False, default=None)
   105→
   106→    @property
   107→    def node(self) -> Node:
   108→        """Return ZWave JS node."""
   109→        return async_get_node_from_entity_id(
   110→            self.hass, self.lock.entity_id, self.ent_reg
   111→        )
   112→
   113→    @functools.cached_property
   114→    def _usercode_cc_version(self) -> int:
   115→        """Return the User Code CC version supported by this node."""
   116→        version = next(
   117→            (
   118→                cc.version
   119→                for cc in self.node.command_classes
   120→                if cc.id == CommandClass.USER_CODE
   121→            ),
   122→            0,
   123→        )
   124→        if version == 0:
   125→            _LOGGER.warning(
   126→                "Lock %s: User Code CC not found on node %s. This may "
   127→                "indicate an incomplete interview. Defaulting to V1 behavior",
   128→                self.lock.entity_id,
   129→                self.node.node_id,
   130→            )
   131→            return 1
   132→        return version
   133→
   134→    @property
   135→    def supports_push(self) -> bool:
   136→        """
   137→        Return whether this lock supports push-based updates.
   138→
   139→        Z-Wave JS emits value update events when the cache changes, so we
   140→        subscribe to those instead of polling.
   141→        """
   142→        return True
   143→
   144→    @property
   145→    def supports_code_slot_events(self) -> bool:
   146→        """Return whether this lock supports code slot events."""
   147→        return True
   148→
   149→    @property
   150→    def connection_check_interval(self) -> timedelta | None:
   151→        """Z-Wave JS exposes config entry state changes, so skip polling."""
   152→        return None
   153→
   154→    def _get_client_state(self) -> tuple[bool, str]:
   155→        """Return whether the Z-Wave JS client is ready and a retry reason."""
   156→        if self.lock_config_entry.state != ConfigEntryState.LOADED:
   157→            return False, "config entry not loaded"
   158→
   159→        runtime_data: ZwaveJSData | None = getattr(
   160→            self.lock_config_entry, "runtime_data", None
   161→        )
   162→        client: Client | None = (
   163→            getattr(runtime_data, "client", None) if runtime_data else None
   164→        )
   165→        if not client:
   166→            return False, "Z-Wave JS client not ready"
   167→
   168→        if not client.connected:
   169→            return False, "Z-Wave JS client not connected"
   170→
   171→        if client.driver is None:
   172→            return False, "Z-Wave JS driver not ready"
   173→
   174→        return True, ""
   175→
   176→    def code_slot_in_use(self, code_slot: int) -> bool | None:
   177→        """Return whether a code slot is in use."""
   178→        try:
   179→            return get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)[ATTR_IN_USE]
   180→        except (KeyError, ValueError):
   181→            return None
   182→
   183→    def _get_slot_entity_states(self, code_slot: int) -> tuple[str, str, str] | None:
   184→        """
   185→        Get active state, PIN value, and PIN entity ID for a managed slot.
   186→
   187→        Returns tuple of (active_state, pin_value, pin_entity_id) if the slot is
   188→        managed by LCM and entities exist, None otherwise.
   189→        """
   190→        try:
   191→            config_entry = next(
   192→                entry
   193→                for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
   194→                if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
   195→                and code_slot in (int(s) for s in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {}))
   196→            )
   197→        except StopIteration:
   198→            return None
   199→
   200→        base_unique_id = f"{config_entry.entry_id}|{code_slot}"
   201→        active_entity_id = self.ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
   202→            BINARY_SENSOR_DOMAIN, DOMAIN, f"{base_unique_id}|{ATTR_ACTIVE}"
   203→        )
   204→        pin_entity_id = self.ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
   205→            TEXT_DOMAIN, DOMAIN, f"{base_unique_id}|{CONF_PIN}"
   206→        )
   207→
   208→        if not active_entity_id or not pin_entity_id:
   209→            return None
   210→
   211→        active_state = self.hass.states.get(active_entity_id)
   212→        pin_state = self.hass.states.get(pin_entity_id)
   213→
   214→        if not active_state or not pin_state:
   215→            return None
   216→
   217→        return (active_state.state, pin_state.state, pin_entity_id)
   218→
   219→    def _slot_expects_pin(self, code_slot: int) -> bool:
   220→        """
   221→        Check if LCM expects a PIN on this slot (active=ON with PIN set).
   222→
   223→        Used to ignore stale userIdStatus=AVAILABLE events from locks that report
   224→        old status after a code was successfully set.
   225→        """
   226→        states = self._get_slot_entity_states(code_slot)
   227→        if states is None:
   228→            return False
   229→
   230→        active_state, pin_value, _ = states
   231→        return active_state == STATE_ON and bool(pin_value)
   232→
   233→    def _resolve_pin_if_masked(self, value: str, code_slot: int) -> str | None:
   234→        """
   235→        Resolve a PIN value, looking up expected PIN if masked.
   236→
   237→        Some locks return masked values (all asterisks) instead of the actual PIN.
   238→        This method returns the value as-is if not masked, or looks up the expected
   239→        PIN from LCM entities if masked.
   240→
   241→        """
   242→        slot_in_use = self.code_slot_in_use(code_slot)
   243→
   244→        if value == "0" * len(value) and slot_in_use is False:
   245→            # Some locks return all zeros instead of a blank value when cleared - treat
   246→            # as unmasked cleared value
   247→            return ""
   248→
   249→        # If not masked, return as-is
   250→        if not value or not (value == "*" * len(value) and slot_in_use):
   251→            return value
   252→
   253→        # Masked - look up expected PIN from LCM entities
   254→        # Use ATTR_ACTIVE binary sensor (not CONF_ENABLED switch) to match what
   255→        # sync logic expects. ATTR_ACTIVE considers conditions, so we only resolve
   256→        # masked codes when sync expects a PIN on the lock.
   257→        states = self._get_slot_entity_states(code_slot)
   258→        if states is None:
   259→            return None
   260→
   261→        active_state, pin_value, pin_entity_id = states
   262→
   263→        if active_state == STATE_ON:
   264→            _LOGGER.debug(
   265→                "PIN is masked for lock %s code slot %s, assuming value from PIN entity %s",
   266→                self.lock.entity_id,
   267→                code_slot,
   268→                pin_entity_id,
   269→            )
   270→            return pin_value
   271→
   272→        # Fall back to returning masked value if active state is not ON (e.g. slot not
   273→        # enabled) - we don't care what the value is, just that there is one so that
   274→        # the sync logic treats this slot as having a PIN set on the lock
   275→        return value
   276→
   277→    @callback
   278→    def subscribe_push_updates(self) -> None:
   279→        """Subscribe to User Code CC value update events."""
   280→        # Idempotent - skip if already subscribed
   281→        if self._value_update_unsub is not None:
   282→            return
   283→
   284→        ready, reason = self._get_client_state()
   285→        if not ready:
   286→            _LOGGER.debug(
   287→                "Lock %s: push subscription deferred (%s)",
   288→                self.lock.entity_id,
   289→                reason,
   290→            )
   291→            self._schedule_push_retry(reason)
   292→            return
   293→
   294→        @callback
   295→        def on_value_updated(event: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   296→            """Handle value update events from Z-Wave JS."""
   297→            args: dict[str, Any] = event["args"]
   298→            # Filter for User Code CC - handle both userCode and userIdStatus
   299→            if args.get("commandClass") != CommandClass.USER_CODE:
   300→                return
   301→
   302→            property_name = args.get("property")
   303→            if property_name not in (
   304→                LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY,
   305→                LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY,
   306→            ):
   307→                return
   308→
   309→            code_slot = int(args["propertyKey"])
   310→
   311→            # Slot 0 is not a valid user code slot (used for status/metadata)
   312→            if code_slot == 0:
   313→                return
   314→
   315→            # Clear in-progress tracking only on userCode updates for the slot
   316→            # we were setting. userIdStatus updates don't confirm acceptance and
   317→            # could race with duplicate-code notifications.
   318→            if (
   319→                property_name == LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY
   320→                and code_slot == self._set_in_progress_code_slot
   321→            ):
   322→                self._set_in_progress_code_slot = None
   323→
   324→            # Handle userIdStatus updates - slot cleared is a fast path
   325→            if property_name == LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY:
   326→                status = args.get("newValue")
   327→                if status == CodeSlotStatus.AVAILABLE:
   328→                    # Ignore AVAILABLE status if LCM expects a PIN on this slot.
   329→                    # Some locks send stale AVAILABLE events after a code was set,
   330→                    # which would cause infinite sync loops.
   331→                    if self._slot_expects_pin(code_slot):
   332→                        _LOGGER.debug(
   333→                            "Lock %s: ignoring userIdStatus=AVAILABLE for slot %s "
   334→                            "(LCM expects PIN on this slot)",
   335→                            self.lock.entity_id,
   336→                            code_slot,
   337→                        )
   338→                        return
   339→
   340→                    # Slot was cleared - update coordinator if needed
   341→                    if self.coordinator and self.coordinator.data.get(code_slot) != "":
   342→                        _LOGGER.debug(
   343→                            "Lock %s: slot %s userIdStatus=AVAILABLE, marking cleared",
   344→                            self.lock.entity_id,
   345→                            code_slot,
   346→                        )
   347→                        self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: ""})
   348→                return
   349→
   350→            # Handle userCode updates
   351→            # newValue is the raw PIN string (or None/empty/"0000" if cleared)
   352→            new_value = args.get("newValue")
   353→            # Treat empty, None, or all-zeros as cleared
   354→            if not new_value or (
   355→                isinstance(new_value, str) and new_value.strip("0") == ""
   356→            ):
   357→                value = ""
   358→            else:
   359→                value = str(new_value)
   360→
   361→            # Resolve masked codes (all asterisks) to expected PIN
   362→            # This prevents infinite sync loops when locks return masked values
   363→            resolved = self._resolve_pin_if_masked(value, code_slot)
   364→            if resolved is None:
   365→                _LOGGER.debug(
   366→                    "Lock %s: skipping masked code update for slot %s "
   367→                    "(unable to resolve)",
   368→                    self.lock.entity_id,
   369→                    code_slot,
   370→                )
   371→                return
   372→
   373→            # Skip if value hasn't changed (Z-Wave JS sends duplicate events)
   374→            if self.coordinator and self.coordinator.data.get(code_slot) == resolved:
   375→                return
   376→
   377→            _LOGGER.debug(
   378→                "Lock %s received push update for slot %s: %s",
   379→                self.lock.entity_id,
   380→                code_slot,
   381→                "****" if resolved else "(cleared)",
   382→            )
   383→
   384→            # Push update to coordinator
   385→            if self.coordinator:
   386→                self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: resolved})
   387→
   388→        try:
   389→            self._value_update_unsub = self.node.on("value updated", on_value_updated)
   390→        except ValueError as err:
   391→            _LOGGER.debug(
   392→                "Lock %s push subscription deferred: %s", self.lock.entity_id, err
   393→            )
   394→            self._schedule_push_retry("node not ready")
   395→
   396→    def _schedule_push_retry(self, reason: str) -> None:
   397→        """Schedule a retry for push subscription if one isn't pending."""
   398→        if self._push_retry_cancel:
   399→            return
   400→
   401→        _LOGGER.debug(
   402→            "Lock %s: scheduling push subscription retry in %ss (%s)",
   403→            self.lock.entity_id,
   404→            PUSH_SUBSCRIBE_RETRY_DELAY,
   405→            reason,
   406→        )
   407→        self._push_retry_cancel = async_call_later(
   408→            self.hass, PUSH_SUBSCRIBE_RETRY_DELAY, self._handle_push_retry
   409→        )
   410→
   411→    @callback
   412→    def _handle_push_retry(self, _now: Any) -> None:
   413→        """Retry push subscription after delay."""
   414→        if self._push_retry_cancel:
   415→            self._push_retry_cancel()
   416→            self._push_retry_cancel = None
   417→        self.subscribe_push_updates()
   418→
   419→    @callback
   420→    def unsubscribe_push_updates(self) -> None:
   421→        """Unsubscribe from value update events."""
   422→        if self._push_retry_cancel:
   423→            self._push_retry_cancel()
   424→            self._push_retry_cancel = None
   425→        if self._value_update_unsub:
   426→            self._value_update_unsub()
   427→            self._value_update_unsub = None
   428→
   429→    @callback
   430→    def _zwave_js_event_filter(self, event_data: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   431→        """Filter out events."""
   432→        # Try to find the lock that we are getting an event for, skipping
   433→        # ones that don't match
   434→        assert self.node.client.driver
   435→        return (
   436→            event_data[ATTR_HOME_ID] == self.node.client.driver.controller.home_id
   437→            and event_data[ATTR_NODE_ID] == self.node.node_id
   438→            and event_data[ATTR_DEVICE_ID] == self.lock.device_id
   439→        )
   440→
   441→    @callback
   442→    def _handle_zwave_js_event(self, evt: Event) -> None:
   443→        """Handle Z-Wave JS event."""
   444→        if evt.data[ATTR_TYPE] != NotificationType.ACCESS_CONTROL:
   445→            _LOGGER.debug(
   446→                "Lock %s received non Access Control event: %s",
   447→                self.lock.entity_id,
   448→                evt.as_dict(),
   449→            )
   450→            return
   451→
   452→        params = evt.data.get(ATTR_PARAMETERS) or {}
   453→        code_slot = params.get("userId", 0)
   454→
   455→        # Handle duplicate code rejection — only when LCM initiated the set
   456→        if (
   457→            evt.data[ATTR_EVENT]
   458→            == AccessControlNotificationEvent.NEW_USER_CODE_NOT_ADDED_DUE_TO_DUPLICATE_CODE
   459→            and self._set_in_progress_code_slot is not None
   460→            and code_slot in (0, self._set_in_progress_code_slot)
   461→        ):
   462→            slot = self._set_in_progress_code_slot or code_slot
   463→            self.hass.async_create_task(
   464→                self._async_handle_duplicate_code(),
   465→                f"Handle duplicate code for {self.lock.entity_id} slot {slot}",
   466→            )
   467→            return
   468→
   469→        self.async_fire_code_slot_event(
   470→            code_slot=code_slot,
   471→            to_locked=next(
   472→                (
   473→                    to_locked
   474→                    for to_locked, codes in ACCESS_CONTROL_NOTIFICATION_TO_LOCKED.items()
   475→                    if evt.data[ATTR_EVENT] in codes
   476→                ),
   477→                None,
   478→            ),
   479→            action_text=evt.data.get(ATTR_EVENT_LABEL),
   480→            source_data=evt,
   481→        )
   482→
   483→    async def _async_handle_duplicate_code(self) -> None:
   484→        """Handle duplicate code rejection from the lock."""
   485→        lock_ent_id = self.lock.entity_id
   486→        code_slot = self._set_in_progress_code_slot
   487→        self._set_in_progress_code_slot = None
   488→        if code_slot is None:
   489→            return
   490→        try:
   491→            # Only move forward if a config entry manages this lock and code slot - there
   492→            # can only be one due to our config entry uniqueness requirement.
   493→            entry = next(
   494→                entry
   495→                for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
   496→                if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
   497→                and code_slot in (int(s) for s in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {}))
   498→            )
   499→        except StopIteration:
   500→            return
   501→
   502→        # Guard that will never trigger but satisfies type checkers that an entity exists
   503→        if (
   504→            enabled_ent_id := self.ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
   505→                SWITCH_DOMAIN, DOMAIN, f"{entry.entry_id}|{code_slot}|{CONF_ENABLED}"
   506→            )
   507→        ) is None:
   508→            return
   509→
   510→        await self.hass.services.async_call(
   511→            SWITCH_DOMAIN,
   512→            SERVICE_TURN_OFF,
   513→            {ATTR_ENTITY_ID: enabled_ent_id},
   514→            blocking=True,
   515→        )
   516→
   517→        entry_title = entry.title or entry.entry_id
   518→
   519→        def _dupe_msg(
   520→            lock_entity_id: str, code_slot: str, config_entry_title: str
   521→        ) -> str:
   522→            """Return message for duplicate code rejection."""
   523→            return (
   524→                f"Lock {lock_entity_id} rejected the code for slot {code_slot} "
   525→                f"(config entry {config_entry_title}) because it duplicates a code "
   526→                f"in another slot. Slot {code_slot} has been disabled. Please set a "
   527→                f"unique code and re-enable slot {code_slot}"
   528→            )
   529→
   530→        _LOGGER.error(
   531→            _dupe_msg("%s", "%s", "%s"),
   532→            lock_ent_id,
   533→            code_slot,
   534→            entry_title,
   535→            code_slot,
   536→            code_slot,
   537→        )
   538→
   539→        async_create(
   540→            self.hass,
   541→            _dupe_msg(f"**{lock_ent_id}**", f"**{code_slot}**", f"**{entry_title}**"),
   542→            title="Duplicate Lock Code Rejected",
   543→            notification_id=(f"{DOMAIN}_{lock_ent_id}_{code_slot}_dupe"),
   544→        )
   545→
   546→    @property
   547→    def domain(self) -> str:
   548→        """Return integration domain."""
   549→        return ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN
   550→
   551→    async def async_setup(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
   552→        """Set up lock by provider."""
   553→        self._listeners.append(
   554→            self.hass.bus.async_listen(
   555→                ZWAVE_JS_NOTIFICATION_EVENT,
   556→                self._handle_zwave_js_event,
   557→                self._zwave_js_event_filter,
   558→            )
   559→        )
   560→
   561→    async def async_unload(self, remove_permanently: bool) -> None:
   562→        """Unload lock."""
   563→        for listener in self._listeners:
   564→            listener()
   565→        self._listeners.clear()
   566→        await super().async_unload(remove_permanently)
   567→
   568→    async def async_is_integration_connected(self) -> bool:
   569→        """Return whether the Z-Wave JS client is connected."""
   570→        ready, _reason = self._get_client_state()
   571→        return ready
   572→
   573→    async def async_is_device_available(self) -> bool:
   574→        """Return whether the Z-Wave node is available for commands."""
   575→        try:
   576→            return self.node.status != NodeStatus.DEAD
   577→        except Exception as err:  # noqa: BLE001
   578→            _LOGGER.debug(
   579→                "Lock %s: failed to check device availability: %s",
   580→                self.lock.entity_id,
   581→                err,
   582→            )
   583→            return False
   584→
   585→    async def async_hard_refresh_codes(self) -> dict[int, str | None]:
   586→        """
   587→        Perform hard refresh and return all codes.
   588→
   589→        Uses Z-Wave JS's refresh_cc_values which handles checksum optimization
   590→        internally - it will skip re-fetching codes if the checksum hasn't changed.
   591→        Returns codes in the same format as async_get_usercodes().
   592→        """
   593→        await self._async_refresh_usercode_cache()
   594→        return await self.async_get_usercodes()
   595→
   596→    async def async_set_usercode(
   597→        self, code_slot: int, usercode: str, name: str | None = None
   598→    ) -> bool:
   599→        """
   600→        Set a usercode on a code slot.
   601→
   602→        Returns True if the value was changed, False if already set to this value.
   603→        """
   604→        # Check if the code is already set to this value (avoid unnecessary network call)
   605→        try:
   606→            if (current := get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)).get("in_use"):
   607→                current_code = str(current.get("usercode", ""))
   608→                # Resolve masked codes and compare
   609→                if usercode == self._resolve_pin_if_masked(current_code, code_slot):
   610→                    _LOGGER.debug(
   611→                        "Lock %s slot %s already has this PIN, skipping set",
   612→                        self.lock.entity_id,
   613→                        code_slot,
   614→                    )
   615→                    return False
   616→        except Exception:
   617→            # If we can't check the cache, proceed with the set
   618→            pass
   619→
   620→        self._set_in_progress_code_slot = code_slot
   621→        service_data = {
   622→            ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self.lock.entity_id,
   623→            ATTR_CODE_SLOT: code_slot,
   624→            ATTR_USERCODE: usercode,
   625→        }
   626→        await self.async_call_service(
   627→            ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_SET_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
   628→        )
   629→        # V1 locks don't reliably update the Z-Wave JS value cache after set.
   630→        # Poll the slot directly from the device to force-update the cache
   631→        # before the coordinator reads it, preventing sync loops.
   632→        # No try-except: if the poll fails, we must not proceed with the
   633→        # optimistic update since async_request_refresh() would overwrite it
   634→        # with stale cache data, reintroducing the sync loop. Letting the
   635→        # error propagate allows the sync mechanism to retry the operation.
   636→        if self._usercode_cc_version < 2:
   637→            await get_usercode_from_node(self.node, code_slot)
   638→        # Optimistic update: Z-Wave command succeeded (lock acknowledged), but the
   639→        # value cache updates asynchronously via push notification. Update coordinator
   640→        # immediately to prevent sync loops from reading stale cache data.
   641→        if self.coordinator:
   642→            self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: usercode})
   643→        return True
   644→
   645→    async def async_clear_usercode(self, code_slot: int) -> bool:
   646→        """
   647→        Clear a usercode on a code slot.
   648→
   649→        Returns True if the value was changed, False if already cleared.
   650→        """
   651→        # Check if the slot is already cleared (avoid unnecessary network call)
   652→        try:
   653→            current = get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)
   654→            if not current.get("in_use"):
   655→                _LOGGER.debug(
   656→                    "Lock %s slot %s already cleared, skipping clear",
   657→                    self.lock.entity_id,
   658→                    code_slot,
   659→                )
   660→                return False
   661→        except Exception:
   662→            # If we can't check the cache, proceed with the clear
   663→            pass
   664→
   665→        service_data = {
   666→            ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self.lock.entity_id,
   667→            ATTR_CODE_SLOT: code_slot,
   668→        }
   669→        await self.async_call_service(
   670→            ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_CLEAR_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
   671→        )
   672→        # V1 locks don't reliably update the Z-Wave JS value cache after clear.
   673→        # Poll the slot directly from the device to force-update the cache
   674→        # before the coordinator reads it, preventing sync loops.
   675→        # See comment in async_set_usercode for why this is not wrapped in
   676→        # try-except.
   677→        if self._usercode_cc_version < 2:
   678→            await get_usercode_from_node(self.node, code_slot)
   679→        # Optimistic update: Z-Wave command succeeded (lock acknowledged), but the
   680→        # value cache updates asynchronously via push notification. Update coordinator
   681→        # immediately to prevent sync loops from reading stale cache data.
   682→        if self.coordinator:
   683→            self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: ""})
   684→        return True
   685→
   686→    def _get_usercodes_from_cache(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   687→        """Get usercodes from Z-Wave JS value DB cache."""
   688→        try:
   689→            return list(get_usercodes(self.node) or [])
   690→        except Exception as err:
   691→            raise LockDisconnected from err
   692→
   693→    async def _async_refresh_usercode_cache(self) -> None:
   694→        """Refresh usercode cache from the device."""
   695→        try:
   696→            await self.node.async_refresh_cc_values(CommandClass.USER_CODE)
   697→        except Exception as err:
   698→            raise LockDisconnected from err
   699→
   700→    async def async_get_usercodes(self) -> dict[int, str | None]:
   701→        """Get dictionary of code slots and usercodes."""
   702→        code_slots = {
   703→            int(code_slot)
   704→            for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
   705→            for code_slot in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {})
   706→            if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
   707→        }
   708→        data: dict[int, str | None] = {}
   709→
   710→        if not await self.async_is_integration_connected():
   711→            raise LockDisconnected
   712→
   713→        slots = self._get_usercodes_from_cache()
   714→        slots_by_num = {int(slot["code_slot"]): slot for slot in slots}
   715→
   716→        # If any configured slot is missing or has unknown state, do one hard
   717→        # refresh to populate the cache. This is more efficient than fetching
   718→        # individual slots and uses Z-Wave JS's checksum optimization.
   719→        # Note: We call _async_refresh_usercode_cache directly here to avoid
   720→        # recursion since async_hard_refresh_codes calls async_get_usercodes.
   721→        if any(
   722→            slot_num not in slots_by_num or slots_by_num[slot_num].get("in_use") is None
   723→            for slot_num in code_slots
   724→        ):
   725→            _LOGGER.debug(
   726→                "Lock %s has missing/unknown slots, performing hard refresh",
   727→                self.lock.entity_id,
   728→            )
   729→            await self._async_refresh_usercode_cache()
   730→            slots = self._get_usercodes_from_cache()
   731→            slots_by_num = {int(slot["code_slot"]): slot for slot in slots}
   732→
   733→        for slot in slots:
   734→            code_slot = int(slot["code_slot"])
   735→            usercode: str = slot["usercode"] or ""
   736→            in_use: bool | None = slot["in_use"]
   737→
   738→            if not in_use:
   739→                data[code_slot] = ""
   740→            # Resolve PIN (handles masked codes), skip if unresolvable
   741→            elif resolved := self._resolve_pin_if_masked(usercode, code_slot):
   742→                data[code_slot] = resolved
   743→
   744→        slots_with_pin = [s for s, v in data.items() if v]
   745→        slots_not_enabled = [s for s, v in data.items() if not v]
   746→        _LOGGER.debug(
   747→            "Lock %s: %s slots with PIN %s, %s slots not enabled %s",
   748→            self.lock.entity_id,
   749→            len(slots_with_pin),
   750→            slots_with_pin,
   751→            len(slots_not_enabled),
   752→            slots_not_enabled,
   753→        )
   754→        return data
   755→

"""Z-Wave JS lock provider.

Handles push updates, masked PIN resolution, duplicate code detection, and rate-limited
set/clear operations. See ARCHITECTURE.md for the provider's role in the data flow.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import timedelta
import functools
import logging
from typing import Any

from zwave_js_server.client import Client
from zwave_js_server.const import CommandClass, NodeStatus
from zwave_js_server.const.command_class.lock import (
    ATTR_CODE_SLOT,
    ATTR_IN_USE,
    ATTR_USERCODE,
    LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY,
    LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY,
    CodeSlotStatus,
)
from zwave_js_server.const.command_class.notification import (
    AccessControlNotificationEvent,
    NotificationType,
)
from zwave_js_server.model.node import Node
from zwave_js_server.util.lock import (
    get_usercode,
    get_usercode_from_node,
    get_usercodes,
)

from homeassistant.components.binary_sensor import DOMAIN as BINARY_SENSOR_DOMAIN
from homeassistant.components.persistent_notification import async_create
from homeassistant.components.switch import DOMAIN as SWITCH_DOMAIN
from homeassistant.components.text import DOMAIN as TEXT_DOMAIN
from homeassistant.components.zwave_js.const import (
    ATTR_EVENT,
    ATTR_EVENT_LABEL,
    ATTR_HOME_ID,
    ATTR_NODE_ID,
    ATTR_PARAMETERS,
    ATTR_TYPE,
    DOMAIN as ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN,
    SERVICE_CLEAR_LOCK_USERCODE,
    SERVICE_SET_LOCK_USERCODE,
    ZWAVE_JS_NOTIFICATION_EVENT,
)
from homeassistant.components.zwave_js.helpers import async_get_node_from_entity_id
from homeassistant.components.zwave_js.models import ZwaveJSData
from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry, ConfigEntryState
from homeassistant.const import (
    ATTR_DEVICE_ID,
    ATTR_ENTITY_ID,
    CONF_ENABLED,
    CONF_PIN,
    SERVICE_TURN_OFF,
    STATE_ON,
)
from homeassistant.core import Event, callback
from homeassistant.helpers.event import async_call_later

from ..const import ATTR_ACTIVE, CONF_LOCKS, CONF_SLOTS, DOMAIN
from ..data import get_entry_data
from ..exceptions import LockDisconnected
from ._base import BaseLock

_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)
PUSH_SUBSCRIBE_RETRY_DELAY = 10

# All known Access Control Notification CC events that indicate the lock is locked
# or unlocked
ACCESS_CONTROL_NOTIFICATION_TO_LOCKED = {
    True: (
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.AUTO_LOCK_LOCKED_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.KEYPAD_LOCK_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.LOCK_OPERATION_WITH_USER_CODE,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.LOCKED_BY_RF_WITH_INVALID_USER_CODE,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.MANUAL_LOCK_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.RF_LOCK_OPERATION,
    ),
    False: (
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.KEYPAD_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.MANUAL_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.RF_UNLOCK_OPERATION,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.UNLOCK_BY_RF_WITH_INVALID_USER_CODE,
        AccessControlNotificationEvent.UNLOCK_OPERATION_WITH_USER_CODE,
    ),
}


@dataclass(repr=False, eq=False)
class ZWaveJSLock(BaseLock):
    """Class to represent ZWave JS lock."""

    lock_config_entry: ConfigEntry = field(repr=False)
    _listeners: list[Callable[[], None]] = field(init=False, default_factory=list)
    _value_update_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = field(init=False, default=None)
    _push_retry_cancel: Callable[[], None] | None = field(init=False, default=None)
    _set_in_progress_code_slot: int | None = field(init=False, default=None)

    @property
    def node(self) -> Node:
        """Return ZWave JS node."""
        return async_get_node_from_entity_id(
            self.hass, self.lock.entity_id, self.ent_reg
        )

    @functools.cached_property
    def _usercode_cc_version(self) -> int:
        """Return the User Code CC version supported by this node."""
        version = next(
            (
                cc.version
                for cc in self.node.command_classes
                if cc.id == CommandClass.USER_CODE
            ),
            0,
        )
        if version == 0:
            _LOGGER.warning(
                "Lock %s: User Code CC not found on node %s. This may "
                "indicate an incomplete interview. Defaulting to V1 behavior",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                self.node.node_id,
            )
            return 1
        return version

    @property
    def supports_push(self) -> bool:
        """
        Return whether this lock supports push-based updates.

        Z-Wave JS emits value update events when the cache changes, so we
        subscribe to those instead of polling.
        """
        return True

    @property
    def supports_code_slot_events(self) -> bool:
        """Return whether this lock supports code slot events."""
        return True

    @property
    def connection_check_interval(self) -> timedelta | None:
        """Z-Wave JS exposes config entry state changes, so skip polling."""
        return None

    def _get_client_state(self) -> tuple[bool, str]:
        """Return whether the Z-Wave JS client is ready and a retry reason."""
        if self.lock_config_entry.state != ConfigEntryState.LOADED:
            return False, "config entry not loaded"

        runtime_data: ZwaveJSData | None = getattr(
            self.lock_config_entry, "runtime_data", None
        )
        client: Client | None = (
            getattr(runtime_data, "client", None) if runtime_data else None
        )
        if not client:
            return False, "Z-Wave JS client not ready"

        if not client.connected:
            return False, "Z-Wave JS client not connected"

        if client.driver is None:
            return False, "Z-Wave JS driver not ready"

        return True, ""

    def code_slot_in_use(self, code_slot: int) -> bool | None:
        """Return whether a code slot is in use."""
        try:
            return get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)[ATTR_IN_USE]
        except (KeyError, ValueError):
            return None

    def _get_slot_entity_states(self, code_slot: int) -> tuple[str, str, str] | None:
        """
        Get active state, PIN value, and PIN entity ID for a managed slot.

        Returns tuple of (active_state, pin_value, pin_entity_id) if the slot is
        managed by LCM and entities exist, None otherwise.
        """
        try:
            config_entry = next(
                entry
                for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
                if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
                and code_slot in (int(s) for s in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {}))
            )
        except StopIteration:
            return None

        base_unique_id = f"{config_entry.entry_id}|{code_slot}"
        active_entity_id = self.ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
            BINARY_SENSOR_DOMAIN, DOMAIN, f"{base_unique_id}|{ATTR_ACTIVE}"
        )
        pin_entity_id = self.ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
            TEXT_DOMAIN, DOMAIN, f"{base_unique_id}|{CONF_PIN}"
        )

        if not active_entity_id or not pin_entity_id:
            return None

        active_state = self.hass.states.get(active_entity_id)
        pin_state = self.hass.states.get(pin_entity_id)

        if not active_state or not pin_state:
            return None

        return (active_state.state, pin_state.state, pin_entity_id)

    def _slot_expects_pin(self, code_slot: int) -> bool:
        """
        Check if LCM expects a PIN on this slot (active=ON with PIN set).

        Used to ignore stale userIdStatus=AVAILABLE events from locks that report
        old status after a code was successfully set.
        """
        states = self._get_slot_entity_states(code_slot)
        if states is None:
            return False

        active_state, pin_value, _ = states
        return active_state == STATE_ON and bool(pin_value)

    def _resolve_pin_if_masked(self, value: str, code_slot: int) -> str | None:
        """
        Resolve a PIN value, looking up expected PIN if masked.

        Some locks return masked values (all asterisks) instead of the actual PIN.
        This method returns the value as-is if not masked, or looks up the expected
        PIN from LCM entities if masked.

        """
        slot_in_use = self.code_slot_in_use(code_slot)

        if value == "0" * len(value) and slot_in_use is False:
            # Some locks return all zeros instead of a blank value when cleared - treat
            # as unmasked cleared value
            return ""

        # If not masked, return as-is
        if not value or not (value == "*" * len(value) and slot_in_use):
            return value

        # Masked - look up expected PIN from LCM entities
        # Use ATTR_ACTIVE binary sensor (not CONF_ENABLED switch) to match what
        # sync logic expects. ATTR_ACTIVE considers conditions, so we only resolve
        # masked codes when sync expects a PIN on the lock.
        states = self._get_slot_entity_states(code_slot)
        if states is None:
            return None

        active_state, pin_value, pin_entity_id = states

        if active_state == STATE_ON:
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "PIN is masked for lock %s code slot %s, assuming value from PIN entity %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                code_slot,
                pin_entity_id,
            )
            return pin_value

        # Fall back to returning masked value if active state is not ON (e.g. slot not
        # enabled) - we don't care what the value is, just that there is one so that
        # the sync logic treats this slot as having a PIN set on the lock
        return value

    @callback
    def subscribe_push_updates(self) -> None:
        """Subscribe to User Code CC value update events."""
        # Idempotent - skip if already subscribed
        if self._value_update_unsub is not None:
            return

        ready, reason = self._get_client_state()
        if not ready:
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s: push subscription deferred (%s)",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                reason,
            )
            self._schedule_push_retry(reason)
            return

        @callback
        def on_value_updated(event: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
            """Handle value update events from Z-Wave JS."""
            args: dict[str, Any] = event["args"]
            # Filter for User Code CC - handle both userCode and userIdStatus
            if args.get("commandClass") != CommandClass.USER_CODE:
                return

            property_name = args.get("property")
            if property_name not in (
                LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY,
                LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY,
            ):
                return

            code_slot = int(args["propertyKey"])

            # Slot 0 is not a valid user code slot (used for status/metadata)
            if code_slot == 0:
                return

            # Clear in-progress tracking only on userCode updates for the slot
            # we were setting. userIdStatus updates don't confirm acceptance and
            # could race with duplicate-code notifications.
            if (
                property_name == LOCK_USERCODE_PROPERTY
                and code_slot == self._set_in_progress_code_slot
            ):
                self._set_in_progress_code_slot = None

            # Handle userIdStatus updates - slot cleared is a fast path
            if property_name == LOCK_USERCODE_STATUS_PROPERTY:
                status = args.get("newValue")
                if status == CodeSlotStatus.AVAILABLE:
                    # Ignore AVAILABLE status if LCM expects a PIN on this slot.
                    # Some locks send stale AVAILABLE events after a code was set,
                    # which would cause infinite sync loops.
                    if self._slot_expects_pin(code_slot):
                        _LOGGER.debug(
                            "Lock %s: ignoring userIdStatus=AVAILABLE for slot %s "
                            "(LCM expects PIN on this slot)",
                            self.lock.entity_id,
                            code_slot,
                        )
                        return

                    # Slot was cleared - update coordinator if needed
                    if self.coordinator and self.coordinator.data.get(code_slot) != "":
                        _LOGGER.debug(
                            "Lock %s: slot %s userIdStatus=AVAILABLE, marking cleared",
                            self.lock.entity_id,
                            code_slot,
                        )
                        self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: ""})
                return

            # Handle userCode updates
            # newValue is the raw PIN string (or None/empty/"0000" if cleared)
            new_value = args.get("newValue")
            # Treat empty, None, or all-zeros as cleared
            if not new_value or (
                isinstance(new_value, str) and new_value.strip("0") == ""
            ):
                value = ""
            else:
                value = str(new_value)

            # Resolve masked codes (all asterisks) to expected PIN
            # This prevents infinite sync loops when locks return masked values
            resolved = self._resolve_pin_if_masked(value, code_slot)
            if resolved is None:
                _LOGGER.debug(
                    "Lock %s: skipping masked code update for slot %s "
                    "(unable to resolve)",
                    self.lock.entity_id,
                    code_slot,
                )
                return

            # Skip if value hasn't changed (Z-Wave JS sends duplicate events)
            if self.coordinator and self.coordinator.data.get(code_slot) == resolved:
                return

            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s received push update for slot %s: %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                code_slot,
                "****" if resolved else "(cleared)",
            )

            # Push update to coordinator
            if self.coordinator:
                self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: resolved})

        try:
            self._value_update_unsub = self.node.on("value updated", on_value_updated)
        except ValueError as err:
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s push subscription deferred: %s", self.lock.entity_id, err
            )
            self._schedule_push_retry("node not ready")

    def _schedule_push_retry(self, reason: str) -> None:
        """Schedule a retry for push subscription if one isn't pending."""
        if self._push_retry_cancel:
            return

        _LOGGER.debug(
            "Lock %s: scheduling push subscription retry in %ss (%s)",
            self.lock.entity_id,
            PUSH_SUBSCRIBE_RETRY_DELAY,
            reason,
        )
        self._push_retry_cancel = async_call_later(
            self.hass, PUSH_SUBSCRIBE_RETRY_DELAY, self._handle_push_retry
        )

    @callback
    def _handle_push_retry(self, _now: Any) -> None:
        """Retry push subscription after delay."""
        if self._push_retry_cancel:
            self._push_retry_cancel()
            self._push_retry_cancel = None
        self.subscribe_push_updates()

    @callback
    def unsubscribe_push_updates(self) -> None:
        """Unsubscribe from value update events."""
        if self._push_retry_cancel:
            self._push_retry_cancel()
            self._push_retry_cancel = None
        if self._value_update_unsub:
            self._value_update_unsub()
            self._value_update_unsub = None

    @callback
    def _zwave_js_event_filter(self, event_data: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        """Filter out events."""
        # Try to find the lock that we are getting an event for, skipping
        # ones that don't match
        assert self.node.client.driver
        return (
            event_data[ATTR_HOME_ID] == self.node.client.driver.controller.home_id
            and event_data[ATTR_NODE_ID] == self.node.node_id
            and event_data[ATTR_DEVICE_ID] == self.lock.device_id
        )

    @callback
    def _handle_zwave_js_event(self, evt: Event) -> None:
        """Handle Z-Wave JS event."""
        if evt.data[ATTR_TYPE] != NotificationType.ACCESS_CONTROL:
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s received non Access Control event: %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                evt.as_dict(),
            )
            return

        params = evt.data.get(ATTR_PARAMETERS) or {}
        code_slot = params.get("userId", 0)

        # Handle duplicate code rejection — only when LCM initiated the set
        if (
            evt.data[ATTR_EVENT]
            == AccessControlNotificationEvent.NEW_USER_CODE_NOT_ADDED_DUE_TO_DUPLICATE_CODE
            and self._set_in_progress_code_slot is not None
            and code_slot in (0, self._set_in_progress_code_slot)
        ):
            slot = self._set_in_progress_code_slot or code_slot
            self.hass.async_create_task(
                self._async_handle_duplicate_code(),
                f"Handle duplicate code for {self.lock.entity_id} slot {slot}",
            )
            return

        self.async_fire_code_slot_event(
            code_slot=code_slot,
            to_locked=next(
                (
                    to_locked
                    for to_locked, codes in ACCESS_CONTROL_NOTIFICATION_TO_LOCKED.items()
                    if evt.data[ATTR_EVENT] in codes
                ),
                None,
            ),
            action_text=evt.data.get(ATTR_EVENT_LABEL),
            source_data=evt,
        )

    async def _async_handle_duplicate_code(self) -> None:
        """Handle duplicate code rejection from the lock."""
        lock_ent_id = self.lock.entity_id
        code_slot = self._set_in_progress_code_slot
        self._set_in_progress_code_slot = None
        if code_slot is None:
            return
        try:
            # Only move forward if a config entry manages this lock and code slot - there
            # can only be one due to our config entry uniqueness requirement.
            entry = next(
                entry
                for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
                if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
                and code_slot in (int(s) for s in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {}))
            )
        except StopIteration:
            return

        # Guard that will never trigger but satisfies type checkers that an entity exists
        if (
            enabled_ent_id := self.ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
                SWITCH_DOMAIN, DOMAIN, f"{entry.entry_id}|{code_slot}|{CONF_ENABLED}"
            )
        ) is None:
            return

        await self.hass.services.async_call(
            SWITCH_DOMAIN,
            SERVICE_TURN_OFF,
            {ATTR_ENTITY_ID: enabled_ent_id},
            blocking=True,
        )

        entry_title = entry.title or entry.entry_id

        def _dupe_msg(
            lock_entity_id: str, code_slot: str, config_entry_title: str
        ) -> str:
            """Return message for duplicate code rejection."""
            return (
                f"Lock {lock_entity_id} rejected the code for slot {code_slot} "
                f"(config entry {config_entry_title}) because it duplicates a code "
                f"in another slot. Slot {code_slot} has been disabled. Please set a "
                f"unique code and re-enable slot {code_slot}"
            )

        _LOGGER.error(
            _dupe_msg("%s", "%s", "%s"),
            lock_ent_id,
            code_slot,
            entry_title,
            code_slot,
            code_slot,
        )

        async_create(
            self.hass,
            _dupe_msg(f"**{lock_ent_id}**", f"**{code_slot}**", f"**{entry_title}**"),
            title="Duplicate Lock Code Rejected",
            notification_id=(f"{DOMAIN}_{lock_ent_id}_{code_slot}_dupe"),
        )

    @property
    def domain(self) -> str:
        """Return integration domain."""
        return ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN

    async def async_setup(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
        """Set up lock by provider."""
        self._listeners.append(
            self.hass.bus.async_listen(
                ZWAVE_JS_NOTIFICATION_EVENT,
                self._handle_zwave_js_event,
                self._zwave_js_event_filter,
            )
        )

    async def async_unload(self, remove_permanently: bool) -> None:
        """Unload lock."""
        for listener in self._listeners:
            listener()
        self._listeners.clear()
        await super().async_unload(remove_permanently)

    async def async_is_integration_connected(self) -> bool:
        """Return whether the Z-Wave JS client is connected."""
        ready, _reason = self._get_client_state()
        return ready

    async def async_is_device_available(self) -> bool:
        """Return whether the Z-Wave node is available for commands."""
        try:
            return self.node.status != NodeStatus.DEAD
        except Exception as err:  # noqa: BLE001
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s: failed to check device availability: %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                err,
            )
            return False

    async def async_hard_refresh_codes(self) -> dict[int, str | None]:
        """
        Perform hard refresh and return all codes.

        Uses Z-Wave JS's refresh_cc_values which handles checksum optimization
        internally - it will skip re-fetching codes if the checksum hasn't changed.
        Returns codes in the same format as async_get_usercodes().
        """
        await self._async_refresh_usercode_cache()
        return await self.async_get_usercodes()

    async def async_set_usercode(
        self, code_slot: int, usercode: str, name: str | None = None
    ) -> bool:
        """
        Set a usercode on a code slot.

        Returns True if the value was changed, False if already set to this value.
        """
        # Check if the code is already set to this value (avoid unnecessary network call)
        try:
            if (current := get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)).get("in_use"):
                current_code = str(current.get("usercode", ""))
                # Resolve masked codes and compare
                if usercode == self._resolve_pin_if_masked(current_code, code_slot):
                    _LOGGER.debug(
                        "Lock %s slot %s already has this PIN, skipping set",
                        self.lock.entity_id,
                        code_slot,
                    )
                    return False
        except Exception:
            # If we can't check the cache, proceed with the set
            pass

        self._set_in_progress_code_slot = code_slot
        service_data = {
            ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self.lock.entity_id,
            ATTR_CODE_SLOT: code_slot,
            ATTR_USERCODE: usercode,
        }
        await self.async_call_service(
            ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_SET_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
        )
        # V1 locks don't reliably update the Z-Wave JS value cache after set.
        # Poll the slot directly from the device to force-update the cache
        # before the coordinator reads it, preventing sync loops.
        # No try-except: if the poll fails, we must not proceed with the
        # optimistic update since async_request_refresh() would overwrite it
        # with stale cache data, reintroducing the sync loop. Letting the
        # error propagate allows the sync mechanism to retry the operation.
        if self._usercode_cc_version < 2:
            await get_usercode_from_node(self.node, code_slot)
        # Optimistic update: Z-Wave command succeeded (lock acknowledged), but the
        # value cache updates asynchronously via push notification. Update coordinator
        # immediately to prevent sync loops from reading stale cache data.
        if self.coordinator:
            self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: usercode})
        return True

    async def async_clear_usercode(self, code_slot: int) -> bool:
        """
        Clear a usercode on a code slot.

        Returns True if the value was changed, False if already cleared.
        """
        # Check if the slot is already cleared (avoid unnecessary network call)
        try:
            current = get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)
            if not current.get("in_use"):
                _LOGGER.debug(
                    "Lock %s slot %s already cleared, skipping clear",
                    self.lock.entity_id,
                    code_slot,
                )
                return False
        except Exception:
            # If we can't check the cache, proceed with the clear
            pass

        service_data = {
            ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self.lock.entity_id,
            ATTR_CODE_SLOT: code_slot,
        }
        await self.async_call_service(
            ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_CLEAR_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
        )
        # V1 locks don't reliably update the Z-Wave JS value cache after clear.
        # Poll the slot directly from the device to force-update the cache
        # before the coordinator reads it, preventing sync loops.
        # See comment in async_set_usercode for why this is not wrapped in
        # try-except.
        if self._usercode_cc_version < 2:
            await get_usercode_from_node(self.node, code_slot)
        # Optimistic update: Z-Wave command succeeded (lock acknowledged), but the
        # value cache updates asynchronously via push notification. Update coordinator
        # immediately to prevent sync loops from reading stale cache data.
        if self.coordinator:
            self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: ""})
        return True

    def _get_usercodes_from_cache(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get usercodes from Z-Wave JS value DB cache."""
        try:
            return list(get_usercodes(self.node) or [])
        except Exception as err:
            raise LockDisconnected from err

    async def _async_refresh_usercode_cache(self) -> None:
        """Refresh usercode cache from the device."""
        try:
            await self.node.async_refresh_cc_values(CommandClass.USER_CODE)
        except Exception as err:
            raise LockDisconnected from err

    async def async_get_usercodes(self) -> dict[int, str | None]:
        """Get dictionary of code slots and usercodes."""
        code_slots = {
            int(code_slot)
            for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
            for code_slot in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {})
            if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
        }
        data: dict[int, str | None] = {}

        if not await self.async_is_integration_connected():
            raise LockDisconnected

        slots = self._get_usercodes_from_cache()
        slots_by_num = {int(slot["code_slot"]): slot for slot in slots}

        # If any configured slot is missing or has unknown state, do one hard
        # refresh to populate the cache. This is more efficient than fetching
        # individual slots and uses Z-Wave JS's checksum optimization.
        # Note: We call _async_refresh_usercode_cache directly here to avoid
        # recursion since async_hard_refresh_codes calls async_get_usercodes.
        if any(
            slot_num not in slots_by_num or slots_by_num[slot_num].get("in_use") is None
            for slot_num in code_slots
        ):
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s has missing/unknown slots, performing hard refresh",
                self.lock.entity_id,
            )
            await self._async_refresh_usercode_cache()
            slots = self._get_usercodes_from_cache()
            slots_by_num = {int(slot["code_slot"]): slot for slot in slots}

        for slot in slots:
            code_slot = int(slot["code_slot"])
            usercode: str = slot["usercode"] or ""
            in_use: bool | None = slot["in_use"]

            if not in_use:
                data[code_slot] = ""
            # Resolve PIN (handles masked codes), skip if unresolvable
            elif resolved := self._resolve_pin_if_masked(usercode, code_slot):
                data[code_slot] = resolved

        slots_with_pin = [s for s, v in data.items() if v]
        slots_not_enabled = [s for s, v in data.items() if not v]
        _LOGGER.debug(
            "Lock %s: %s slots with PIN %s, %s slots not enabled %s",
            self.lock.entity_id,
            len(slots_with_pin),
            slots_with_pin,
            len(slots_not_enabled),
            slots_not_enabled,
        )
        return data

1→"""Abstract base for lock providers.
     2→
     3→Handles rate limiting, connection checking, and coordinator refresh after operations.
     4→See ARCHITECTURE.md for the provider interface contract.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from __future__ import annotations
     8→
     9→import asyncio
    10→from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable
    11→from dataclasses import dataclass, field

    13→import functools

    19→from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry, ConfigEntryState

    21→from homeassistant.core import Event, HomeAssistant, State, callback

    23→from homeassistant.helpers import device_registry as dr, entity_registry as er
    24→from homeassistant.helpers.update_coordinator import UpdateFailed

    26→from ..const import (
    27→    ATTR_ACTION_TEXT,
    28→    ATTR_CODE_SLOT,
    29→    ATTR_CODE_SLOT_NAME,
    30→    ATTR_EXTRA_DATA,
    31→    ATTR_FROM,
    32→    ATTR_LCM_CONFIG_ENTRY_ID,
    33→    ATTR_LOCK_CONFIG_ENTRY_ID,
    34→    ATTR_NOTIFICATION_SOURCE,
    35→    ATTR_TO,
    36→    CONF_LOCKS,
    37→    CONF_SLOTS,
    38→    DOMAIN,
    39→    EVENT_LOCK_STATE_CHANGED,
    40→)
    41→from ..coordinator import LockUsercodeUpdateCoordinator
    42→from ..data import get_entry_data
    43→from ..exceptions import (
    44→    DuplicateCodeError,
    45→    LockDisconnected,
    46→    ProviderNotImplementedError,
    47→)
    48→from .const import LOGGER
    49→
    50→MIN_OPERATION_DELAY = 2.0
    51→_OPERATION_MESSAGES: dict[Literal["get", "set", "clear", "refresh"], str] = {
    52→    "get": "get from",
    53→    "set": "set on",
    54→    "clear": "clear on",
    55→    "refresh": "hard refresh",
    56→}
    57→
    58→
    59→@dataclass(repr=False, eq=False)
    60→class BaseLock:
    61→    """
    62→    Base class for lock provider implementations.
    63→
    64→    Data Fetching Modes
    65→    -------------------
    66→    The coordinator supports three update modes. All modes include an initial poll.
    67→
    68→    1. Poll for updates (default):
    69→       - Periodic calls to get_usercodes() at usercode_scan_interval
    70→       - Used when supports_push = False
    71→       - Suitable for integrations without real-time events
    72→
    73→    2. Push for updates:
    74→       - Real-time value updates via subscribe_push_updates()
    75→       - Enabled when supports_push = True
    76→       - Disables periodic polling (poll for updates)
    77→       - Updates pushed via coordinator.push_update({slot: value})
    78→
    79→    3. Poll for drift:
    80→       - Periodic hard_refresh_codes() at hard_refresh_interval
    81→       - Detects out-of-band changes (e.g., codes changed at keypad)
    82→       - Runs regardless of push/poll mode
    83→       - Set hard_refresh_interval = None to disable
    84→
    85→    4. Poll connection state:
    86→       - Periodic async_internal_is_integration_connected() at connection_check_interval
    87→       - Helps detect reconnects for integrations without config entry state signals
    88→       - Set connection_check_interval = None to disable
    89→
    90→    Configuration Examples
    91→    ----------------------
    92→    Poll-only (default):
    93→        supports_push = False
    94→        usercode_scan_interval = timedelta(minutes=1)
    95→        hard_refresh_interval = None
    96→        connection_check_interval = timedelta(seconds=30)
    97→
    98→    Push with drift detection (recommended for Z-Wave JS):
    99→        supports_push = True
   100→        hard_refresh_interval = timedelta(hours=1)
   101→        connection_check_interval = None
   102→        # Override subscribe_push_updates() to handle value events
   103→
   104→    Poll with drift detection:
   105→        supports_push = False
   106→        usercode_scan_interval = timedelta(minutes=1)
   107→        hard_refresh_interval = timedelta(hours=1)
   108→        connection_check_interval = timedelta(seconds=30)
   109→
   110→    Exception Handling
   111→    ------------------
   112→    Provider implementations should raise LockCodeManagerError (or subclasses like
   113→    LockDisconnected) for lock communication failures. The coordinator catches
   114→    LockCodeManagerError and handles it appropriately (e.g., retrying, logging).
   115→
   116→    Do NOT raise generic exceptions or HomeAssistantError directly - always use
   117→    LCM-derived exceptions so the coordinator can distinguish lock failures from
   118→    other errors.
   119→    """
   120→
   121→    hass: HomeAssistant = field(repr=False)
   122→    dev_reg: dr.DeviceRegistry = field(repr=False)
   123→    ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry = field(repr=False)
   124→    lock_config_entry: ConfigEntry | None = field(repr=False)
   125→    lock: er.RegistryEntry
   126→    device_entry: dr.DeviceEntry | None = field(default=None, init=False)
   127→    coordinator: LockUsercodeUpdateCoordinator | None = field(default=None, init=False)
   128→    _aio_lock: asyncio.Lock = field(default_factory=asyncio.Lock, init=False)
   129→    _last_operation_time: float = field(default=0.0, init=False)
   130→    _min_operation_delay: float = field(default=MIN_OPERATION_DELAY, init=False)
   131→    _last_connection_up: bool | None = field(default=None, init=False)
   132→    _config_entry_state_unsub: Callable[[], None] | None = field(
   133→        default=None, init=False
   134→    )
   135→    _last_entry_state: ConfigEntryState | None = field(default=None, init=False)
   136→    _setup_complete: asyncio.Event = field(default_factory=asyncio.Event, init=False)
   137→
   138→    @final
   139→    async def _async_executor_call(
   140→        self, func: Callable[..., Any], *args: Any, **kwargs: Any
   141→    ) -> Any:
   142→        """Run a sync method in the executor."""
   143→        if kwargs:
   144→            return await self.hass.async_add_executor_job(
   145→                functools.partial(func, *args, **kwargs)
   146→            )
   147→        return await self.hass.async_add_executor_job(func, *args)
   148→
   149→    @final
   150→    async def _execute_rate_limited(
   151→        self,
   152→        operation_type: Literal["get", "set", "clear", "refresh"],
   153→        func: Callable[..., Awaitable[Any]],
   154→        *args: Any,
   155→        pre_execute: Callable[[], None] | None = None,
   156→        **kwargs: Any,
   157→    ) -> Any:
   158→        """Execute operation with connection check, serialization, and delay.
   159→
   160→        pre_execute runs inside the lock before the operation, for checks
   161→        that must be atomic with the operation (e.g., duplicate detection).
   162→        """
   163→        if not await self.async_internal_is_integration_connected():
   164→            raise LockDisconnected(
   165→                f"Cannot {_OPERATION_MESSAGES[operation_type]} {self.lock.entity_id} - integration not connected"
   166→            )
   167→
   168→        if not await self.async_is_device_available():
   169→            raise LockDisconnected(
   170→                f"Cannot {_OPERATION_MESSAGES[operation_type]} {self.lock.entity_id} - device not available"
   171→            )
   172→
   173→        async with self._aio_lock:
   174→            if pre_execute:
   175→                pre_execute()
   176→
   177→            elapsed = time.monotonic() - self._last_operation_time
   178→            if elapsed < self._min_operation_delay:
   179→                delay = self._min_operation_delay - elapsed
   180→                LOGGER.debug(
   181→                    "Rate limiting %s operation on %s, waiting %.1f seconds",
   182→                    operation_type,
   183→                    self.lock.entity_id,
   184→                    delay,
   185→                )
   186→                await asyncio.sleep(delay)
   187→
   188→            LOGGER.debug(
   189→                "Executing %s operation on %s",
   190→                operation_type,
   191→                self.lock.entity_id,
   192→            )
   193→
   194→            result = await func(*args, **kwargs)
   195→            self._last_operation_time = time.monotonic()
   196→            return result
   197→
   198→    @final
   199→    @callback
   200→    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
   201→        """Post initialization."""
   202→        if not (device_id := self.lock.device_id):
   203→            return
   204→        self.device_entry = self.dev_reg.async_get(device_id)
   205→
   206→    @final
   207→    def __repr__(self) -> str:
   208→        """Return string representation of self."""
   209→        return f"{self.__class__.__name__}(domain={self.domain}, lock={self.lock.entity_id})"
   210→
   211→    @final
   212→    def __hash__(self) -> int:

   228→    def mask_pin(self, pin: str | None) -> str:
   229→        """Return a masked representation of a PIN for logging."""
   230→        from ..util import mask_pin  # noqa: PLC0415
   231→
   232→        return mask_pin(

   238→    @staticmethod
   239→    def is_masked_or_empty(code: str | None) -> bool:
   240→        """Return whether a code is masked or empty (not comparable)."""
   241→        if code is None or code == "":
   242→            return True
   243→        code_str = str(code)
   244→        return code_str == "*" * len(code_str)

   246→    @final
   247→    def is_slot_managed(self, code_slot: int) -> bool:
   248→        """Return whether a code slot is managed by any LCM config entry for this lock."""
   249→        return any(
   250→            self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
   251→            and code_slot in (int(s) for s in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {}))
   252→            for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
   253→        )
   254→
   255→    @final
   256→    def _check_duplicate_code(self, code_slot: int, usercode: str) -> None:
   257→        """Raise DuplicateCodeError if the PIN duplicates another slot on this lock."""
   258→        # Return early if there's nothing to check
   259→        if not usercode or not self.coordinator or not self.coordinator.data:
   260→            return
   261→        try:
   262→            other_code_slot = next(
   263→                other_code_slot
   264→                for other_code_slot, other_usercode in self.coordinator.data.items()
   265→                if other_code_slot != code_slot
   266→                and not self.is_masked_or_empty(other_usercode)
   267→                and str(other_usercode) == usercode
   268→            )
   269→        except StopIteration:
   270→            pass
   271→        else:
   272→            raise DuplicateCodeError(
   273→                code_slot=code_slot,
   274→                conflicting_slot=other_code_slot,
   275→                conflicting_slot_managed=self.is_slot_managed(other_code_slot),
   276→                lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
   277→            )

   309→    @property
   310→    def supports_push(self) -> bool:
   311→        """
   312→        Return whether this lock supports push-based updates.
   313→
   314→        When True, the lock will receive real-time value updates via
   315→        subscribe_push_updates() instead of periodic polling. Polling is
   316→        still used for initial load and drift detection (hard_refresh_interval).

   321→    def supports_code_slot_events(self) -> bool:

   324→
   325→        When True, the lock can fire events indicating which code slot was used
   326→        to lock/unlock. This affects the event entity's event_types - locks that
   327→        support this will have their entity_id included in event_types.

   334→    @callback
   335→    def subscribe_push_updates(self) -> None:
   336→        """
   337→        Subscribe to push-based value updates.
   338→
   339→        Override in subclasses that support push. Called during async_setup()
   340→        when supports_push is True, and retried during drift detection if
   341→        initial setup failed.
   342→
   343→        Implementations MUST be idempotent (no-op if already subscribed).
   344→        """
   345→        self._raise_not_implemented(
   346→            "subscribe_push_updates",
   347→            "Override this method to subscribe to real-time value updates "
   348→            "and call coordinator.push_update({slot: value}) when updates arrive. "
   349→            "Must be idempotent (no-op if already subscribed).",
   350→        )
   351→
   352→    @callback
   353→    def unsubscribe_push_updates(self) -> None:
   354→        """
   355→        Unsubscribe from push-based value updates.
   356→
   357→        Implementations MUST be idempotent (no-op if already unsubscribed).
   358→
   359→        Override in subclasses that support push. Called during async_unload()
   360→        when supports_push is True.
   361→        """
   362→        self._raise_not_implemented(
   363→            "unsubscribe_push_updates",
   364→            "Override this method to clean up any subscriptions "
   365→            "created in subscribe_push_updates().",
   366→        )

   368→    @final
   369→    async def async_setup_internal(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
   370→        """
   371→        Set up lock and coordinator, signaling completion to waiters.
   372→
   373→        Provider-specific async_setup() runs first so providers can initialize
   374→        any state the coordinator needs during its first refresh. Then the base
   375→        setup creates the coordinator and subscribes to push updates.
   376→        """
   377→        try:
   378→            await self.async_setup(config_entry)
   379→            await self._async_setup_internal(config_entry)
   380→        finally:
   381→            self._setup_complete.set()
   382→
   383→    @final
   384→    async def _async_setup_internal(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
   385→        """Set up lock and coordinator."""
   386→        lock_entity_id = self.lock.entity_id
   387→        # Track the provider's config entry (e.g., zwave_js) so we can resubscribe
   388→        # when that integration reloads or reconnects.
   389→        self._setup_config_entry_state_listener()
   390→
   391→        # Reuse existing coordinator or create new one
   392→        if self.coordinator is not None:
   393→            self.hass.async_create_task(
   394→                self.coordinator.async_request_refresh(),
   395→                f"Refresh coordinator for {lock_entity_id}",
   396→            )
   397→            return
   398→
   399→        self.coordinator = LockUsercodeUpdateCoordinator(self.hass, self, config_entry)
   400→        if config_entry.state == ConfigEntryState.SETUP_IN_PROGRESS:
   401→            try:
   402→                await self.coordinator.async_config_entry_first_refresh()
   403→            except (ConfigEntryNotReady, UpdateFailed) as err:
   404→                LOGGER.warning(
   405→                    "Failed to fetch initial data for lock %s: %s. "
   406→                    "Entities will be created but unavailable until lock is ready.",
   407→                    lock_entity_id,
   408→                    err,
   409→                )
   410→        else:
   411→            await self.coordinator.async_refresh()
   412→            if not self.coordinator.last_update_success:
   413→                LOGGER.warning(
   414→                    "Failed to fetch initial data for lock %s: %s. "
   415→                    "Entities will be created but unavailable until lock is ready.",
   416→                    lock_entity_id,
   417→                    self.coordinator.last_exception,
   418→                )
   419→
   420→        # Subscribe to push updates after coordinator is ready. If the provider's
   421→        # config entry isn't loaded yet, defer and let the state listener resubscribe.
   422→        if self.supports_push:
   423→            if (
   424→                self.lock_config_entry
   425→                and self.lock_config_entry.state != ConfigEntryState.LOADED
   426→            ):
   427→                LOGGER.debug(
   428→                    "Lock %s: deferring push subscription until config entry is loaded",
   429→                    lock_entity_id,
   430→                )
   431→            else:
   432→                self.subscribe_push_updates()
   433→
   434→    def setup(self) -> None:
   435→        """Set up lock by provider."""
   436→        pass

   438→    async def async_setup(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
   439→        """
   440→        Set up lock by provider.
   441→
   442→        Overridden by providers that need custom one time async setup logic.
   443→        """
   444→        await self.hass.async_add_executor_job(self.setup)
   445→
   446→    @final
   447→    async def async_wait_for_setup(self) -> None:
   448→        """Wait until async_setup has completed."""
   449→        await self._setup_complete.wait()

   451→    def unload(self, remove_permanently: bool) -> None:
   452→        """Unload lock."""
   453→        pass
   454→
   455→    async def async_unload(self, remove_permanently: bool) -> None:
   456→        """Unload lock."""
   457→        if self._config_entry_state_unsub:
   458→            self._config_entry_state_unsub()
   459→            self._config_entry_state_unsub = None
   460→
   461→        # Unsubscribe from push updates before unloading
   462→        if self.supports_push:
   463→            self.unsubscribe_push_updates()
   464→
   465→        await self.hass.async_add_executor_job(self.unload, remove_permanently)
